name: Slide Layout-Template Intent
summary: A slot-based layout-template vocabulary for slides — title, section-divider, two-column, code, image-and-caption, quote — that a named slide fills by projecting content into the template's slots. Every surveyed deck framework (Slidev, mdx-deck, Spectacle) ships this finite set; the standard names the vocabulary and the slot contract per template, not the visual styling (FUI owns the components). Composes webcomponents (slot projection) + webexpressions (slot/section interpolation), and references a slide's layout via the deck document model. A vocabulary contract, not a runtime — the layout an author picks per slide.

template.description: The named layout a slide uses, each exposing a fixed slot set. title = deck/section opener (title + subtitle); section = a divider between sections; two-column = side-by-side regions; code = a focused code surface (composes code-view); image = image with optional caption; quote = a pull-quote with attribution; blank = author-controlled free layout.

content.description: How a slot is filled. slot = light-DOM content projected into a named <slot> (the default, framework-free); expression = interpolated via webexpressions when the slide is data-driven (a for-each over a slide array).

description:
A finite, named vocabulary

Authors do not hand-build slide layouts each time — they pick from a small, recurring set: a title opener, a section divider, a two-column split, a code focus, an image with caption, a quote. Slidev, mdx-deck, and Spectacle all ship exactly this set. The Slide Layout-Template intent names the vocabulary and the slot contract each template exposes, so a slide declares its layout and projects content by slot name.

Slot contract, not styling

Each template exposes named slots (title, subtitle, left/right, code, caption, quote/cite). Content is projected via Shadow-DOM <slot> (framework-free) or interpolated via Web Expressions when slides are data-driven. The visual realization of each template — type scale, spacing, the code surface — is a Frontier UI component concern, not part of this contract.

Composition

  • Document model: a slide references its template via the Deck Document Model's layout field.
  • Code template: composes the code-view block (and its step-through extension).
